name: King David Kalakaua Statue
narration: The route begins near the Kalakaua statue, a gateway marker for the avenue named after Hawaii's last reigning king.
scopeNotes: At the Ala Moana Boulevard/Kalakaua Avenue route start.

name: Luxury Row
narration: Luxury Row condenses Kalakaua's high-end storefront identity into a compact block of international fashion brands.
scopeNotes: Official Kalakaua Avenue frontage.

name: Royal Hawaiian Center / Helumoa
narration: Royal Hawaiian Center sits on Helumoa, the historic coconut-grove landscape now folded into Kalakaua's retail core.
scopeNotes: Kalakaua frontage; heritage source ties the site to Helumoa.

name: Sheraton Waikiki Beach Resort
narration: Sheraton Waikiki is a 1971 beachfront high-rise that once ranked among the world's largest resort hotels.
scopeNotes: Official Kalakaua address and direct Waikiki Beach frontage.

name: The Royal Hawaiian
narration: The Royal Hawaiian opened in 1927 and made its pink beachfront profile one of Waikiki's defining landmarks.
scopeNotes: Historic Kalakaua hotel with Waikiki Beach frontage.

name: Waikiki Shopping Plaza
narration: Waikiki Shopping Plaza has marked the Kalakaua and Seaside retail corner since 1978.
scopeNotes: Kalakaua/Seaside corner on the route.

name: Waikiki Theatre Site
narration: The former Waikiki Theatre was a 1936 atmospheric movie palace with artificial clouds, a rainbow, tropical planting, and a pipe organ.
scopeNotes: Historic building site directly on Kalakaua Avenue.

name: OUTRIGGER Waikiki Beachcomber Hotel
narration: The Beachcomber layers local art, food, and live entertainment into a central Kalakaua hotel block.
scopeNotes: Official Kalakaua Avenue address.

name: International Market Place
narration: International Market Place began as a tiki-era open-air Waikiki marketplace and still centers its identity around a banyan-tree setting.
scopeNotes: Direct Kalakaua entrance.

name: OUTRIGGER Waikiki Beach Resort
narration: Outrigger Waikiki Beach Resort ties Kalakaua's hotel strip directly to Duke's, live music, and Waikiki surf culture.
scopeNotes: Kalakaua address with direct Waikiki Beach frontage.

name: Apuakehau Stream and Waikiki Beach Boys
narration: Near the Outrigger and Moana beach edge, Apuakehau connects the old stream mouth with the Waikiki Beach Boys' surf legacy.
scopeNotes: Historic Trail coordinates place it on the Waikiki Beach edge.

name: Moana Surfrider
narration: Opened in 1901, the Moana Surfrider is Waikiki's original beachfront hotel and still puts its porte cochere on Kalakaua Avenue.
scopeNotes: Historic Kalakaua hotel with Waikiki Beach frontage.

name: Moana Banyan Courtyard
narration: The Moana courtyard banyan was planted in 1904 and now anchors one of Waikiki's oldest oceanfront hotel spaces.
scopeNotes: On the Moana beachfront property.

name: Duke Kahanamoku Statue
narration: Duke Kahanamoku's bronze figure faces Kalakaua from Kuhio Beach, honoring the Waikiki waterman who carried surfing worldwide.
scopeNotes: On Kuhio Beach along Kalakaua Avenue.

name: Kuhio Beach Hula Mound
narration: The Kuhio Beach Hula Mound keeps Hawaiian music and hula visible at the beach edge near the Duke statue.
scopeNotes: WIA places the performance site beachside near the Duke statue.

name: Na Pohaku Ola Kapaemahu a Kapuni
narration: The Kapaemahu stones preserve the story of four Tahitian healers whose healing power was remembered on Waikiki Beach.
scopeNotes: Cultural site on Kuhio/Waikiki Beach immediately off Kalakaua.

name: Hyatt Regency Waikiki Beach Resort and Spa
narration: Hyatt Regency's twin towers sit across Kalakaua from the Duke statue in the dense center of the beach corridor.
scopeNotes: Official Kalakaua address across from Kuhio Beach.

name: Alohilani Resort Oceanarium
narration: Alohilani transformed the former Pacific Beach Hotel around a large lobby Oceanarium steps from Kuhio Beach.
scopeNotes: Official Kalakaua address near Kuhio Beach.

name: Prince Jonah Kuhio Kalanianaole Statue
narration: Prince Kuhio's bronze figure anchors the civic-art stretch of Kuhio Beach Park on Kalakaua.
scopeNotes: City public-art data places the statue on Kuhio Beach along Kalakaua.
sourceName: City and County of Honolulu Public Art

name: Prince Kuhio Historic Trail Marker
narration: The Prince Kuhio marker ties royal lineage, public service, and Hawaiian Homes advocacy to this beach park.
scopeNotes: Historic marker on the Kalakaua beach edge.

addressOrFrontage: Waikiki Beach from Moana/Kuhio toward Kapahulu
name: Kuhio Beach Park
narration: Kuhio Beach Park is central Waikiki's beach section, shaped by seawalls, groins, public art, and protected swimming water.
scopeNotes: Part of Waikiki Beach directly along Kalakaua.

name: Kuhio Beach Swimming Zone
narration: Kuhio's protected swimming area is bounded by groins, seawall, and shoreline, making this stretch feel calmer than open reef water.
scopeNotes: DLNR describes the exact Kuhio Beach swimming-area boundaries.

name: Waikiki Historic Trail Beach Marker
narration: The Waikiki marker explains the area's older identity as a watery chiefly surf place with named breaks and long rides.
scopeNotes: Historic marker at the Kalakaua/Kapahulu beach end.

name: Makua and Kila
narration: Makua and Kila pairs a boy on a surfboard with a monk seal, giving the Kapahulu end a playful surf-and-sea-life marker.
scopeNotes: City public-art data places the sculpture at Kuhio Beach/Kapahulu.
sourceName: City and County of Honolulu Public Art

name: Surfer on a Wave
narration: Surfer on a Wave turns Waikiki's surf identity into a life-size sculpture visible from the beach sidewalk.
scopeNotes: City public-art data places the sculpture on Kuhio Beach frontage.
sourceName: City and County of Honolulu Public Art

name: Kapahulu Groin / The Wall
narration: The Kapahulu Groin separates Kuhio Beach from the Kapiolani side and helps define the Wall bodyboarding zone.
scopeNotes: Visible shoreline feature at the route end.

addressOrFrontage: Central Waikiki Beach / Royal Hawaiian groin
name: Central Waikiki Beach Nourishment
narration: A 2012 beach project restored a central Waikiki stretch with offshore sand, widening the beach between the Kuhio wall and Royal Hawaiian groin.
scopeNotes: Directly about the engineered shoreline visible from Waikiki Beach.

name: Canoes Surf Break
narration: Canoes is the classic beginner-friendly Waikiki wave offshore of the Royal Hawaiian beach frontage.
scopeNotes: Named surf break directly off Waikiki Beach.

name: Queen's Surf Break
narration: Queen's is an eastern Waikiki reef break tied to the Kuhio and Kapiolani Park edge of the beach.
scopeNotes: Named surf break on the Waikiki Beach edge.

addressOrFrontage: Off Waikiki Aquarium / Queen's Beach edge
name: Publics Reef Break
narration: Publics is a left-hand reef break off the Aquarium end of Waikiki's beach-and-reef corridor.
scopeNotes: Beach-edge surf/reef fact at the Diamond Head end.

addressOrFrontage: Diamond Head end of Waikiki Beach
name: Waikiki Marine Life Conservation District
narration: At Waikiki's Diamond Head end, the Marine Life Conservation District protects reef habitat extending offshore from the Kapahulu groin toward the Natatorium.
scopeNotes: Beach-visible marine conservation zone.

name: Waikiki Aquarium Reef Flat
narration: Just off the Aquarium seawall, a shallow reef flat and dredged channel concentrate fish along the Diamond Head end of Waikiki.
scopeNotes: Direct reef fact for the Waikiki Beach edge.

name: Waikiki Beach Marriott Resort and Spa
narration: Waikiki Beach Marriott stands on a Kalakaua site associated with Queen Liliuokalani's former Waikiki residences.
scopeNotes: Official Kalakaua address near the Kapahulu end.

name: The Twin Fin Hotel
narration: The Twin Fin leans into Waikiki surf culture from the Diamond Head end of Kalakaua near Kuhio Beach.
scopeNotes: Official Kalakaua address near the route end.

addressOrFrontage: Diamond Head end of Waikiki Beach
name: Sans Souci / Kaimana Beach
narration: Sans Souci, also known as Kaimana Beach, is the quieter Diamond Head end of Waikiki's beach corridor.
scopeNotes: Strict beach-corridor item; not on the Kalakaua driving route.

name: Duke's Waikiki
narration: Duke's Waikiki puts food, live music, and Duke Kahanamoku memorabilia directly on the Outrigger's beachfront edge.
scopeNotes: Beachfront restaurant inside Outrigger Waikiki Beach Resort.

name: Hula Grill Waikiki
narration: Hula Grill sits above the Outrigger beach walk, framing local-style food with a direct Waikiki Beach view.
scopeNotes: Beachfront dining at the Outrigger Waikiki Beach Resort address.

name: Monkeypod Kitchen Waikiki
narration: Monkeypod brings a Hawaii regional menu into Royal Hawaiian Center, giving the Kalakaua retail core a food stop with local sourcing cues.
scopeNotes: Located in Royal Hawaiian Center on Kalakaua Avenue.

name: Restaurant Suntory
narration: Restaurant Suntory adds a long-running Japanese dining layer to Royal Hawaiian Center's Kalakaua food scene.
scopeNotes: Official site lists a Kalakaua Avenue Waikiki address.

name: Doraku Sushi Waikiki
narration: Doraku Sushi sits above the Kalakaua and Seaside corner inside Royal Hawaiian Center.
scopeNotes: Source places it in Royal Hawaiian Center with escalator access at Kalakaua and Seaside.

name: STRIPSTEAK Waikiki
narration: STRIPSTEAK brings a Michael Mina steakhouse layer to International Market Place's Grand Lanai above Kalakaua.
scopeNotes: Official site lists International Market Place / Kalakaua Avenue.

name: Lulu's Waikiki
narration: Lulu's sits across from Kuhio Beach at the Diamond Head end of Kalakaua, with an open-air view over the beach edge.
scopeNotes: Official address is on Kalakaua across from Waikiki/Kuhio Beach.

name: Big Island Burger Waikiki
narration: Big Island Burger adds a casual local-beef food stop to Waikiki Shopping Plaza on Kalakaua.
scopeNotes: Official site lists the Waikiki Shopping Plaza / Kalakaua address.

name: Kuhio Beach Hula Show
narration: The Kuhio Beach Hula Show brings free Hawaiian music and hula to the beachside mound on Tuesday and Saturday evenings.
scopeNotes: Recurring event at the beachside hula mound along Kalakaua.

name: Pan-Pacific Festival 2026
narration: The Pan-Pacific Festival returns June 12-14, 2026, with Kalakaua Avenue as the main parade and street-festival spine.
scopeNotes: Official festival page places the parade along Kalakaua Avenue.

name: King Kamehameha Celebration Floral Parade 2026
narration: On June 13, 2026, the King Kamehameha Floral Parade is scheduled to continue onto Kalakaua Avenue before ending near Monsarrat.
scopeNotes: Current event listing names Kalakaua Avenue in the parade route.

name: Aloha Festivals Floral Parade 2026
narration: The 2026 Aloha Festivals Floral Parade is scheduled to travel from Ala Moana Park along Kalakaua Avenue to Kapiolani Park.
scopeNotes: Official event page lists Kalakaua Avenue as the parade route.

name: Royal Hawaiian Center Cultural Programming
narration: Royal Hawaiian Center regularly stages cultural activities such as hula, music, ukulele, lei-making, and keiki programs in the Kalakaua retail core.
recurrence: Recurring cultural activities; verify day/time before narration
scopeNotes: Official events page for a Kalakaua Avenue center.

name: Ahaaina Luau at The Royal Hawaiian
narration: The Royal Hawaiian's Ahaaina luau pairs food, hula, music, and storytelling on the Pink Palace's Waikiki Beach frontage.
recurrence: Mondays and Thursdays per source; verify before live use
scopeNotes: Recurring event at a Kalakaua hotel with beach frontage.

addressOrFrontage: Duke Kahanamoku Beach / western Waikiki Beach
name: Waikiki Friday Fireworks
narration: Friday night fireworks launch from the Hilton Hawaiian Village beachfront and are often visible along western Waikiki Beach.
recurrence: Friday evenings; verify time before narration
scopeNotes: Beach-visible recurring event at the western Waikiki shoreline; not on the Kalakaua driving route.
